rule_text: A trader must implement disciplined exit rules—both to abort losses quickly and to allow profits to run—as the foundation of profitable trading.
entry_conditions: Position established with defined max loss
exit_conditions: Exit immediately if loss reaches -2.0% from entry
Once profit reaches +5.0%, activate trailing stop at 3.0% below peak

rule_text: A trader should enter long positions when price breaks beyond the previous day's normal trading range, as volatility breakouts can generate substantial profits.
entry_conditions: Price closes above (Yesterday's High + 1.5 × Yesterday's Range)
Volume confirms breakout (Volume > 20-day average)
No gap opening (or gap < 0.5 × Yesterday's Range)
exit_conditions: Stop-loss: Yesterday's High
Profit target: Entry + (2 × risk taken)
Time-based exit: 5 bars if no follow-through

Current peak-to-trough drawdown < maximum allowed drawdown
exit_conditions: Exit all open positions when drawdown reaches 35% of account
Analyze whether adverse market regime (not system failure) caused drawdown
Adjust position sizing or resume based on regime analysis

exit_conditions: Reduce position size by 50% until worst-case drawdown <= 10%

rule_text: A trader should seek trades where the profit potential is many multiples of the initial risk (R-multiple), because even with frequent losses, the asymmetric payoff structure can produce net profitability if the trader maintains discipline through consecutive losing trades.
entry_conditions: Trade setup identified with defined stop-loss (initial risk = R)
Profit target calculated such that potential gain ≥ 5.0 × R
exit_conditions: Exit at profit target (5R gain) or initial stop-loss (1R loss)
Abandon system if 5 consecutive losses occur without reviewing edge

rule_text: A trader should stress-test their system against extreme market scenarios (price shocks, gaps, once-in-a-lifetime moves) during development to ensure it can tolerate catastrophic adverse moves.
entry_conditions: System is in backtesting/development phase
Simulate 1-day price shock of 5-7% against position
Simulate 2-day cumulative shock of 8-10% against position
exit_conditions: If simulated loss exceeds account_loss_tolerance_pct, reject system or reduce position sizing
If system cannot survive stress scenario, redesign entry/exit logic or add circuit breakers

Stop loss hit at 1.0x ATR

rule_text: When you predict a directional move in a commodity due to a specific market condition (e.g., supply shortage), exploit the price differential between contract months by taking a directional spread position aligned with that condition's characteristic term structure.
entry_conditions: Supply shortage condition is publicly confirmed or reported
Nearby-to-forward spread is already inverted (backwardation exists)
Spread inversion magnitude exceeds 1.5% of nearby contract price
exit_conditions: Spread returns to contango (forward > nearby)
Initial stop-loss at 3% adverse move in spread
Target: capture 2-3% of spread differential

rule_text: A trader should evaluate and optimize the relationship between win rate (reliability) and profit/loss ratio to achieve positive expectancy, rather than pursuing win rate alone.

understan
rule_text: Enter a trade when a failed test of a previous support or resistance level occurs, as these setups are commonly used entry signals that can trigger profitable trades.

rule_text: A trader should enter a trade expecting a reversal when the market tests a new extreme, then fails that test by closing at the opposite end of the range or showing weakness on a subsequent bar, signaling exhaustion of the trend.
entry_conditions: Market prints a new multi-day extreme (high or low)
On subsequent bar, market closes at opposite end of the previous bar's range
Gap between extreme and current price is at least 50% closed
Current bar shows reversal structure (e.g., pin bar or engulfing opposite polarity)
exit_conditions: Stop loss triggered at 2.5x 50-day ATR above entry
Target: 1.5x ATR from entry in reversal direction
Time-based exit: close trade after 5 bars if target not hit

rule_text: A trader should enter trades only when price action follows a logically sequenced, high-probability setup pattern (trend → retracement → resumption) rather than trading isolated price moves or index movements without understanding the composition of underlying components.

exit_conditions: Exit if retracement extends beyond 78.6%
Exit if component confirmation drops below 50%

rule_text: A trader should enter a breakout position when price forms an inside day (range contraction), as breakouts from such patterns typically offer reliable short-term trading opportunities.

Volatility confirmation: ATR(5) > ATR(20) * 1.2
exit_conditions: Exit on close if ATR(5) contracts back below ATR(20) * 1.0
Exit if price reverses 50% of breakout move

rule_text: A trader should adjust timeframe for entry based on price movement efficiency—using shorter timeframes for clean/fast moves and longer timeframes for noisy/slow moves, as measured by the ratio of net directional movement to total volatility.
entry_conditions: Calculate Kaufman Efficiency Ratio (KER) = |net price change over N periods| / sum of absolute period changes
IF KER >= 0.70: Use 5-minute timeframe for entry signal confirmation
IF KER 0.40-0.69: Use 15-minute timeframe for entry signal confirmation
IF KER < 0.40: Use 60-minute timeframe for entry signal confirmation OR skip setup
exit_conditions: Exit at predetermined stop loss or profit target based on selected timeframe
Exit if efficiency drops below threshold on re-evaluation

Acceleration in opposite direction detected on bar of crossover
exit_conditions: Speed indicator reverses direction again
Price closes below entry support

rule_text: Enter trades when price exhibits acceleration (increasing speed) in the direction of the trend, as accelerating moves are more likely to continue than decelerating or constant-velocity moves.
entry_conditions: Price is moving in a primary direction (trend established)
Current period momentum > previous period momentum by at least 1.5x
Acceleration confirmed for minimum 2 consecutive periods
Entry only after deceleration risk is ruled out (velocity not declining)
exit_conditions: Price decelerates (current momentum < prior momentum)
Velocity crosses below 50-period moving average of velocity

rule_text: Enter a position when price breaks out from a consolidation pattern accompanied by a large increase in volume.
entry_conditions: Price closes above consolidation resistance level
Current bar volume >= 150% of 20-bar average volume during consolidation
Consolidation duration between 12-15 months (longer base = stronger signal)
Pattern is cup-and-handle or double bottom only
exit_conditions: Close below consolidation support level
Stop loss at 2% below breakout level

rule_text: A trader should enter breakout trades only when accompanied by volume significantly above the daily average, as high volume momentum (mass × velocity) increases the probability of sustained directional movement and creates low-risk, high-R-multiple entry opportunities.
entry_conditions: Price breaks above recent resistance/pattern (cup-and-handle, breakout level)
Volume on breakout day exceeds 75% above 20-day average volume
Stop placement at pattern low (narrow stop, low R)
exit_conditions: Close below breakout level on higher volume reversal
Stop loss at pattern low

rule_text: Enter a trade when price acceleration resumes following a deceleration phase after a channel breakout, using tight stops to manage risk while targeting high R-multiple returns.
entry_conditions: Price breaks above established channel
Price decelerates and retraces 38.2% to 50% of breakout move
Acceleration resumes with close above 20-period moving average
Momentum indicator (RSI or MACD) crosses above midline
exit_conditions: Stop-loss hit at 2.0x ATR below entry
Take-profit at 2.0x risk target (1:2 R-multiple)

rule_text: A trader should enter trades on channel breakouts of significant duration combined with fundamental support in the direction predicted by fundamentals, as this reduces whipsaws compared to shorter-term breakouts.
entry_conditions: Price closes above (long) or below (short) the channel high/low
Channel formation period >= 75 days
Fundamental indicators provide strong directional support
Volume confirmation on breakout candle >= 1.5x average volume
exit_conditions: Price re-enters the channel for 3+ consecutive closes
Fundamental thesis breaks

rule_text: A trader should enter when price makes a new extreme after retracing to a reversal setup, using a market-adjusted filter (small for futures/forex, large for equities) added to the extreme price of the prior trend.
entry_conditions: 1-2-3 reversal pattern identified (point 1 = extreme, point 2 = retracement, point 3 = new reversal point)
Price moves toward point 2 and breaks to new extreme
New extreme exceeds prior trend extreme by conservative filter amount
exit_conditions: Exit on reversal of the new extreme
Exit on violation of point 3 (reversal point)

rule_text: Enter trades in the direction aligned with strong fundamental conditions, provided that proper stops, exits, and position sizing are implemented to manage whipsaws and risk.
entry_conditions: Fundamental score >= 0.8 (high conviction fundamentals)
Expert consensus on fundamentals achieved
Price action confirms fundamental direction on daily timeframe
No conflicting technical signals on lower timeframe
exit_conditions: Stop loss at 2x ATR from entry
Exit if fundamentals deteriorate to score < 0.5
Profit target at 4:1 risk-reward ratio

rule_text: A trader should enter on price breakouts calibrated to volatility measures, with channel-based breakouts (40+ days) and intraday volatility breakouts (0.8x ATR) being the most reliable entry techniques.
entry_conditions: Price closes above highest high of the last 50 days (channel breakout)
OR intraday range exceeds 1.0x average true range
Volume confirmation on breakout bar (above 20-day average)
exit_conditions: Stop loss at lowest low of entry bar minus 1 ATR
Take profit at 2:1 risk-reward ratio

rule_text: A protective stop loss functions as an inviolable boundary (like a red light); violating it repeatedly leads to poor outcomes, so a trader should respect and enforce their stop loss discipline consistently.
entry_conditions: Position established with predefined stop loss level
Stop loss calculated as percentage of entry: 1.5% to 2.0% max loss per trade
exit_conditions: Exit immediately when price touches stop loss level with market order
No mental negotiation or moving stop in adverse direction
No override capability

rule_text: A trader should set a stop loss at a defined maximum loss (R) before entering a trade, recognizing that actual average losses will be smaller (approximately 0.5-R), to establish the basis for measuring all trade outcomes as R multiples.
entry_conditions: Trade entry signal is generated
Stop loss is placed at distance = 2.0 × average_recent_loss from entry
exit_conditions: Exit when price closes beyond stop loss level
Initial risk (R) = 2.0 × average loss, actual expected loss ≈ 1.0R

rule_text: A trader should calculate position size based on statistical volatility and risk tolerance, not by simply placing a stop at a fixed dollar amount away from entry price.
entry_conditions: Calculate 2-standard-deviation band around entry price
Apply 20% upward correction to account for right-skewed market distribution
Size position so that adjusted stop represents 0.5% of account equity
exit_conditions: Exit when price closes beyond adjusted 2σ band + 20% correction

rule_text: A trader should exit a position when the stock price retraces against the entry price by a fixed percentage, independent of account equity, to preserve capital through mechanical loss containment.

rule_text: A trader should exit a position when the stock price retraces against the entry price by a fixed percentage, independent of account equity, to preserve capital through mechanical loss containment.
entry_conditions: Position opened at entry price P_entry
Current price P_current < P_entry
exit_conditions: Exit when (P_entry - P_current) / P_entry >= 0.10
OR (P_current / P_entry) <= 0.90

rule_text: A trader should exit a position using a protective stop loss set at a maximum tolerable loss percentage, with the goal of keeping individual losses between 5-8% and averaging all losses around 5-6% over time.
entry_conditions: Position is entered at market price P0
Calculate stop loss threshold = P0 × (1 - 0.05)
exit_conditions: Exit when price falls to or below (P0 × 0.95)
Execute stop order immediately upon breach

Price reverses and closes below point 2 on higher timeframe

exit_conditions: Exit long when price closes below the low of the most recent consolidation
Exit short when price closes above the high of the most recent consolidation

entry_conditions: Position is in an established long-term uptrend or downtrend
Price has consolidated over at least 10 bars

Exit short only when price closes above (consolidation high × (1 + 3.0% buffer))
Move stop up only when new higher consolidation forms (uptrend) or new lower consolidation forms (downtrend)

rule_text: A trader should enter trend-following trades on retracements within established breakout channels, placing stops below the retracement level to capture large R-multiple moves while controlling risk.
entry_conditions: Price breaks above a 40-day high (channel breakout)
Price retraces back by 38-50% of the initial breakout move
Retracement holds above a 20-day moving average
Volume on retracement is lower than breakout volume
exit_conditions: Stop loss triggered below retracement support
Exit when R-multiple target of 3:1 achieved

rule_text: A trader should accept frequent losses if the average reward-to-risk ratio is sufficiently high to produce positive expectancy, measured by win rate multiplied by reward-to-risk ratio.

Average reward ≥ 2.5 × average risk
Expectancy = (win_rate × avg_reward) - ((1 - win_rate) × avg_loss) ≥ $2.00 per $1 risked
exit_conditions: Stop loss at predetermined risk amount
Take profit when avg_reward target reached

exit_conditions: Exit at stop-loss or profit target as per system rules
Re-evaluate cost-to-profit ratio if broker changes commission structure

rule_text: A trader should match their trading frequency and operational demands to their psychological capacity to maintain peak performance; failure to do so introduces execution errors and opportunity costs that can exceed years of profits.
entry_conditions: Trader has completed >= 48 hours rest since last trading session
Trader rates self >= 9/10 on focus and alertness pre-market
No more than 3 consecutive trading days without full day off
Sleep quality previous night >= 7 hours
exit_conditions: Mental fatigue rating drops below 7/10 during session
Execution slippage exceeds 50% of usual tolerance
Exit after maximum 3 consecutive days; then mandatory full rest day

Position size = bankroll / (2% × 1/entry_risk_ratio)
exit_conditions: Exit trade at stop loss (2% of account)
Reduce position size if drawdown exceeds 20%

exit_conditions: Exit trade at stop loss (6% of account)

rule_text: A trader should rigorously test trading systems against historical data to understand what actually works, rather than relying on software that optimizes results to create false confidence in mediocre systems.
entry_conditions: Backtest period includes at least 2 complete market cycles (bull and bear markets)
Out-of-sample test period equals or exceeds 50% of in-sample period
System profitability verified across 3+ distinct market regimes (trending, ranging, high volatility)
Maximum drawdown constraint: accept only systems with max drawdown ≤ 15% of account
exit_conditions: System fails validation in any single market regime
Actual live drawdown exceeds backtested max drawdown by 20%
Win rate in live trading drops below 70% of backtested level

exit_conditions: Broker confirms volume/follow-through met before execution
If volume insufficient, order cancelled without fill

Reduce position size by maximum 25% until new belief framework proves itself over 10 trades
